steps.title: Test Equation I
steps.content: Multiply the given equation $2x + 9y = 7$ by 1.5: $3x + 13.5y = 10.5$. This is identical to Equation I, so both represent the same line — infinitely many solutions. Equation I works.

steps.title: Test Equation II
steps.content: For Equation II ($3x - 13.5y = 10.5$) to be the same line, we'd need a scalar $k$ where $2k=3$ and $9k=-13.5$. The first gives $k=1.5$, the second gives $k=-1.5$ — contradiction. So the lines are not parallel; they intersect at exactly one point. Equation II also works.

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content:
Both Equation I and Equation II can be the second equation in a system with at least one solution.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
